review_title: Useful, but I prefer other similar tools
description: I had a good experience with them. Nevertheless, I wouldn't pay for it. Price is very similar to Ahrefs/SemRush and I would rather invest in those. I would consider it if the tool would be 30%-40% cheaper.
author: Marco Genaro P.
pros: Most of the features are useful. Nothing to say about that. Keyword tracking, reporting, competitors analysis. All is useful either for freelance SEO consultants and agencies either for On-house SEO consultants. What I like the most about Moz if the Domain Authority metric.
cons: I'm not a big fan of their UX, specially in the beginning, I found very hard to find the feature I was looking for. Also, the price is similar to other similar tools that have more and better features (Ahrefs or SemRush).
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Very solid option for All-in-One Seo management in 2025
description: We used Moz Pro for a year after coming across from SE Ranking when they increased their subscription fees substantially. Moz Pro offered a very good experience with an intuitive UI and wide range of features and was a decent alternative to other similarly priced tools. Ultimately though, we preferred SE Ranking for the price bracket.
author: Angus A.
pros: Great UI with a wide range of tools to improve SEO, responsive and easy to setup and track keywords. Cheaper price than the other top tools.
cons: Sometimes the serp analysis data and rankings are not as accurate as your industry leaders like ahrefs and semrush. Also for the price there are other options that may better suit your needs.

review_title: Moz local is overpriced and they make it impossible to cancel!
description: My overall experience with Moz local is bad. I will not use it again.
author: Andrew G.
pros: Moz local at $100 per year iis overpriced in my opinion. I've found a cheaper alternative, BrightLocal that does the same thing for a lower price. When I went to cancel my account, I found that Moz has hidden the "cancel" button somewere. I read the FAQ article that says "Click Continue with cancellation" but this link does not exist. I am unhappy with the service and even more unhappy that Moz has made it impossible to cancel their service without reaching out to them. I think this is an unethical business practice.
cons: What I like least about Moz local is that there is no way to cancel the service.

description: Overall Moz helped us to keep track of various website ranks and was a useful tool in KW research and analysis.
author: Lois O.
pros: Moz is just an authority... it's where I started my SEO journey, as did many other marketers. I like the rank tracking feature and backlink reporting
cons: It's Google only, which isn't the end of the world considering, well... Google! But when their competitors have more data, I've got to count it against them.

review_title: Moz Local is a MUST for any small business owner
description: I've had a great experience with Moz Local over the past 4 years. Nothing negative to say, their syndication and reporting and noting of duplicates is great and a must have for any small business owner.
author: Joshua T.
pros: This is insanely easy to setup and use and the reporting and information it provides a small business owner is great. I work with clients that range from 200+ locations down to 1 location and this software is perfect for small business owners. The reporting is quick and easy and clients love being able to login and see most everything in 2-3 clicks.
cons: I wish they could automate some of their process for bigger clients, but the reason they are so cost friendly is because you have to do "some" manual work to clean up duplicates.

review_title: A great high-level SEO platform for Marketing Executives
description: For over a year I have run side-by-side comparisons of both SEM and Moz. I have run reports on my clients' websites, used the platform for new-business purposes, and have used Moz extensively for keyword research. On all these fronts Moz excelled. I would happily recommend it to any CMO/Marketing Director-level person who's trying to build a strategy. But if you're a small agency trying to figure out which subscription to keep, it might be SEM Rush only for the ability to drill down deeper. I personally didn't enjoy using SEM Rush compared to Moz. Moz gave me information with a minimal of annoyance. Moz is built for the big picture. Between SEM Rush and Moz? Moz will make you feel like a genious.
author: Amy S.
pros: Moz has a really lovely and user-friendly interface that makes it easy for me to pull great-looking reports for clients and for myself. Moz natively generates just the right amount of detail -- not too much or too little -- to make it a great selling tool for Account Directors or in-house Marketing Directors. And it's intuitive enough that you can step away from it for a month or two and go back in and find everything you need in just a few minutes. That said, this platform also offers up lots of detail. I've definitely done some deep-dive keyword research using Moz alongside SEM Rush and been very pleased with what Mox came back with.
cons: The complaint I would make against Moz is the same one I would make against SEM Rush: These are tools that seem to be made for IT/web developers more than marketing directors. It's hard to get a CMO-level report out of either of them. That said, I would definitely say Moz is more "client-friendly." Honestly, SEM Rush and Moz excel at different things, and they're both expensive. If push comes to shove, you may choose SEM Rush for its breadth of data, but then berate yourself for losing the user-friendliness of Moz.

description: It is a solid bit of kit and their rank tracker is as good as any.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I think Moz is one of the easier SEO products to use, the interface is simple / obvious and provides you with all of the info you need to analyse a site's performance.
cons: Moz has many great features but there are others offered by others that provide a wider scope for digital marketing.

description: Our company is a digital marketing company so we utilize a lot of the available SEO tools and Moz is one of the favorites among my team.
author: Zev S.
pros: There are a lot of features offered and data is provided very quickly. You can easily set up and store different campaigns and a lot of insights available.
cons: Not a lot of customization in the reporting. There are also a lot of error messages that occur when utilizing the different features of the site.

description: It's not a bad solution at all. I tried it because of its popularity and I went back to the usual solution I use. Perhaps had I given it more time I would have found more value.
author: Katlego T.
pros: I was using SEMRush and Moz Pro simultaneously and I believe the user dashboard is a little intimidating but overall it is a great product.
cons: The dashboard can be a bit complex for a first time user. It's not as intuitive as other competing solutions.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The biggest thing Moz has going is its name. It earned credibility for being around for so long. The accuracy of these tools is notoriously not fail-proof. I don't know which is the most accurate, but throughout the years, I noticed discrepancies between Moz's results compared to other SEO tools.
author: Sean C.
pros: It's one of the first SEO tools, and as a result, it's more reliable. The Keyword research tool is easy to use.
cons: 1. The freemium model is very limited 2. It's not always reliable. On more than one occasion, the keyword and traffic stats weren't accurate

description: Good experience, but I wish it has more in-depth competitor tracking in terms of different regions/markets.
author: Marmi L.
pros: SEO tracking and multi-campaign management
cons: Limited competitor tracking for different regions

review_title: An SEOs bread and butter - but has been struggling since the migrated their server
description: An essential tool for an SEO. A few niggly bits but it remains my weapon of choice for SEO tasks because of a nice set of tools and a clean, clutter-free UI.
author: Santosh P.
pros: The clean, clutter-free UX and the SEO tools such as page optimisation scores and rankings etc. are excellent. The monthly report setup is easy and straight forward and the crawls are easy to execute. A good package overall. A big shoutout to the customer support team - [SENSITIVE CONTENT] is a joy to interact with and is always ready to help whenever there is an issue. The technical SEO issues Moz identifies are excellent and reliable. Our technical SEO audits would not be possible without Moz.
cons: The weekly updates of Search Visibility are not ideal. Ranking data can sometimes take more than a week to populate which is not ideal when you are on a deadline finishing an audit. Also, since their migrated their servers a couple of months or so ago, their crawls and data population have been flaky with crawls getting stuck etc. Most recently, the PDF report generation has been an issue which has not been ideal. Moz is our primary tool and we count on its reliability. Here's hoping the software stabilises again, soon. And yes - Moz's rogerbot cannot crawl Javascript sites which is not ideal. But then, I am not sure if any SEO tool other than ScreamingFrog can crawl Javascript sites. Here is hoping this feature gets added soon!

author: Claire B.
pros: Moz allows me to easily identify and prioritise keywords to create content for, track and improve rankings for existing content and monitor competition. The interface is easy to use and there are lots of guides on getting the most out of the platform and SEO strategy.
cons: Some of the results in True Competitor aren't accurate and comparing more than 3 would be helpful.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The platform is robust and even after using it for a while, I'm still finding new features to explore. It definitely gives me the info I need to help improve my SEO ranking.
cons: Finding things in the navigation set up can be a little challenging.

review_title: Moz Pro is one of the best website and SEO analyzers on the market
description: Moz Pro provides detailed insights into your website's performance in organic search results by tracking keyword rankings, link metrics, domain authority scores, and more.
author: Edward N.
pros: This suite of tools includes the Moz Bar, Link Explorer, and On-Page Grader. The Moz Bar, in particular, is crucial: it provides powerful insights into the performance of your website and helps you identify potential opportunities.
cons: While it's not a cheap service, the basic version has enough functions to meet most users' needs. For those who need more robust tools and features, there are several more powerful alternatives on the market.

review_title: An SEO Tool That Is Unbeatable
author: Arheer G.
pros: 1. Helps you research new keywords that can lead to organic traffic growth.2. Helps you get content ideas to create monthly content strategy.3. Identifies gaps in traffic for you and your competitors.
cons: There are other SEO tools that can perform similar task to Moz but are available at a much cheaper price.

description: I've used Moz across several roles in the last 4 years and found it to be very useful. My favourite tool from Moz has to be Open Site Explorer, which is great for getting a quick overview of the authority of a website, with data to back this up which mainly comes from backlink profile and social shares. This tools allows me to dig into my backlinks, checking the authority at domain level & page level, along with a spam score which is useful when looking to identify low quality links. it can also be used to compare link metrics with other websites, which is great for discovering new links. I have to say, I've never been a huge fan of using Moz for SEO campaigns, as a lot of the suggestions that were being made to optimise my pages were very basic. However, it looks like they have made a lot of changes since I last used it, providing more of a dashboard approach which is good for getting an overview of a website.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Access to Mos's suit of tools to help with the main aspects of online marketing Great for getting a snapshot of website performance Good for identifying new opportunities for links & Keywords OSE is a great tool which provides a snapshot of your link profile with many great features Nice custom reports Access to a number of discounts or exclusive free trials through Moz's 'Perks'
cons: OSE doesn't discover as many links as some of the other SEO tools that I use

description: The problem is two fold. We need to know how to be smartest with our content strategy, and we also need to present what we're doing and how we're doing it. Moz has made both of those things easier.
author: CJ P.
pros: There's a ton of tools for Keyword research, planning, and strategy. Moz is the easiest to use and the most powerful from a strategy and planning perspective. It gives you all the tools you need in a way that's easy to understand, and easy to explain to known analysts.
cons: It would be nice if it was more plug-and-play for presentations. Other than that, it's really simple to use and that's honestly my ONLY complaint.

description: Its the best tool for keyword research and backlinks audit. It is very easy to use and gives you accurate analysis of data.
author: Adnan A.
pros: The best thing about this tool is that t provides you the most accurate KD of any keyword as compared to other tools..
cons: It does not provide the accurate search volume of keyword as it does in KD

review_title: A complete tool to monitor and improve the SEO strategy for your projects.
author: Sergio O.
pros: What I like the most is that is pretty easy to use. I've been using it for a year and my experience is quite good so far because it offers lots of functionalities and it adapts well to different projects. It offers lots of Keywords indicators that allow you to take better decisions. It also gives you advice, new ideas and ways to improve the SEO with keywords. Moz is also really useful analyzing your competitors because it tells you how strong are your domain and your website, based on numerous parameters.
cons: Pricing is a bit expensive. It's not very accurate in terms of your rank. I mean, it doesn't tell you the exact number where you are in the search results, it only tells you the range where you belong. It also has limitations on the number of competitors it allows you to compare. This depends on the pricing. It doesn't give you immediate feedback on the changes that you make, you have to wait at least one week to check the results.

description: Super easy to use, particularly the backlink research tool, competitor analysis and domain authority tool.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Moz Pro is easy to use. I like using this product for back link research, competitor insights, domain authority, rank tracking, as well as local listing audits.
cons: The keyword research tool can be a bit wonky, and it often fails to load. I also wish that there were more customizations available for reports.

description: I'm an SEO export and I've been using Yext for about 4 years. We use it for our franchisee clients that don't want to pay for premium services like Yext.
author: Vladimir D.
pros: Price. You can easily sync any updates for your business with Moz Local. The platform is very simple and straight forward. I would recommend this tool for business owners with a small budget.
cons: Technical issues. I have not the best experience with Moz Local, especially with connecting Moz Local to Google My Business. I would love to see more available directories for sync.

description: I really like it and think this would be beneficial if you're working in SEO at all. While that's not the focus area of my business, I've found it helpful both personally and for client work. Moz is not super technical so it's easy to get up to speed and using it quickly.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best parts are their Open Site Explorer and Keyword Explorer. I use it pretty frequently to check for backlinks on client sites, and once you're needing it pretty often it only makes sense to pay for Moz Pro. Keyword Explorer is super helpful for improving relevance and structure of website copy.
cons: Definitely an emphasis on SEO over PPC or other functionality, but for me, this wasn't a big deal as that is my preference anyway.

review_title: Moz pro keeps up with the constantly evolving SEO industry.
description: Best back link assessment tool on the market.
author: Talmage E.
pros: Moz pro is an exceptional SEO tool. It is so beneficial to have access it's indexing. It is the first tool I check to understand a websites authority. They coined the term "domain authority". They keep their index up-to-date and publicise each new index. I'm never worried about getting out dated data. I can also trust that their tools will always be on the cutting edge of new trends and processes.
cons: Their keyword research tool seems to be lacking to me. For me, it hasn't been great at helping me with keyword research or even checking for accurate search volumes.

review_title: MOZ.com = Software created by SEO experts
description: MOZ.com (formerly SEOMoz.com) provides SEO software, tools and training. What I like most about MOZ is that I trust their results. Additionally, I always look to their thought leadership and training on any topic related to SEO, SEM, PPC, Keyword Research, Local SEO, etc.
author: Robert E. F.
pros: MOZ is feature-rich with multiple modules and the ability to track performance of multiple websites. Products include: -Moz Pro - Search Marketing: -Moz Local - Local Search Marketing -Moz Content - Content Marketing -Followerwonk - Twitter Analytics -Mozscape API
cons: MOZ, although feature rich, is somewhat expensive for the base package. If you want the other modules like Keyword Research, Backlink Reports, Local SEO, you have to pay extra for each.
